content:
# Revelation Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, page 278 | **Source Image:** REV_001_page_001.jpg

1. [The] revelation of Iesous Christos, which the God gave to him, to show to the slaves of him [the things] which must come to be in swiftness, and he signified [it] having sent through the angel of him to the slave of him Ioannes,

2. who testified the word of the God and the testimony of Iesous Christos, as many things as he saw.

3. Blessed [is] the [one] reading and the [ones] hearing the words of the prophecy and keeping the [things] having been written in it, for the time [is] near.

4. Ioannes to the seven assemblies, the [ones] in the Asia: grace to you and peace from the [one who] is and the [one who] was and the [one who is] coming, and from the seven spirits which [are] before the throne of him,

5. and from Iesous Christos, the witness the faithful, the firstborn of the dead and the ruler of the kings of the earth. To the [one] loving us and having loosed us from the sins of us in the blood of him,

6. and he made us a kingdom, priests to the God and Father of him — to him the glory and the might into the ages of the ages. Amen.

7. Behold, he comes with the clouds, and will see him every eye, and the ones who pierced him, and will beat [their breasts] over him all the tribes of the earth. Yes, amen.

8. I am the Alpha and the Omega, says [the] Lord the God, the [one who] is and the [one who] was and the [one who is] coming, the Almighty.

9. I Ioannes, the brother of you and fellow-partaker in the tribulation and kingdom and endurance in Iesous, I came to be in the island, the [one] being called Patmos, on account of the word of the God and the testimony of Iesous.

10. I came to be in spirit in the lordly day, and I heard behind me a great voice as of a trumpet,

11. saying: What you see, write into a scroll and send to the seven assemblies, to Ephesos and to Smyrna and to Pergamon and to Thyateira and to Sardeis and to Philadelpheia and to Laodikeia.

12. And I turned to see the voice which was speaking with me. And having turned, I saw seven golden lampstands,

13. and in [the] midst of the lampstands [one] like [a] son of man, having been clothed [with a garment] reaching to the feet and having been girded at the breasts [with] a golden belt.

14. And the head of him and the hairs [were] white as white wool, as snow, and the eyes of him as a flame of fire,

15. and the feet of him like fine brass, as having been fired in a furnace, and the voice of him as [the] sound of many waters,

16. and having in the right hand of him seven stars, and out of the mouth of him a sharp two-edged sword proceeding, and the face of him as the sun shines in the power of it.

17. And when I saw him, I fell at the feet of him as dead. And he placed the right [hand] of him upon me, saying: Do not fear. I am the first and the last,

18. and the living [one] — and I became dead, and behold, living I am into the ages of the ages, and I have the keys of the death and of the Hades.

19. Write therefore the [things] which you saw, and the [things] which are, and the [things] which are about to come to be after these [things].

20. The mystery of the seven stars which you saw upon the right [hand] of me, and the seven golden lampstands: the seven stars angels of the seven assemblies are, and the seven lampstands seven assemblies are.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Title (superscription):** Sinaiticus reads ΑΠΟΚΑΛΥΨΙΣ ΙΩΑΝ Γ — "Apocalypse of Ioannes." The numeral Γ (=3) likely refers to a scribal cataloguing number, not a chapter designation.

2. **1:5 — "loosed" (λυσαντι):** Sinaiticus reads ΛΥΣΑΝΤΙ ("having loosed"), not ΛΟΥΣΑΝΤΙ ("having washed") as found in many later manuscripts. This is a significant textual variant. EMET preserves the Sinaiticus reading.

3. **1:6 — "kingdom" (βασιλειαν):** Sinaiticus reads the singular "kingdom" rather than the plural "kings" (βασιλεις) found in some witnesses. The construction "kingdom, priests" preserves the Exodus 19:6 allusion.

4. **1:8 — Speaker ambiguity:** The manuscript does not explicitly identify whether the speaker of v.8 is God or Christ. The appellation "the Almighty" (ΠΑΝΤΟΚΡΑΤΩΡ) typically refers to the Father in Revelation.

5. **1:11 — Expanded list:** Sinaiticus includes the full list of seven assemblies in v.11, which some later manuscripts abbreviate.

6. **1:13 — "son of man":** The Greek reads ΥΙΟΝ ΑΝΘΡΩΠΟΥ without the article — "a son of man" rather than "the Son of Man," preserving the Danielic allusion (Dan 7:13 LXX) while maintaining the anarthrous construction.

7. **1:15 — "fine brass" (χαλκολιβανω):** This rare word ΧΑΛΚΟΛΙΒΑΝΩ appears almost exclusively in Revelation. Its exact meaning is debated — possibly a copper-zinc alloy or burnished bronze. EMET renders literally as "fine brass."

8. **General:** The scriptio continua of Sinaiticus presents the text without word divisions. Verse divisions follow the traditional numbering for reference purposes. Sinaiticus shows characteristic nomina sacra abbreviations throughout (ΘΥ for ΘΕΟΥ, ΙΥ ΧΥ for ΙΗΣΟΥ ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ, etc.).

— EMET
